bids.reasoning: The negotiation has just started (t=0.0). The opponent proposed an offer consisting entirely of `valueE`, which provides the absolute minimum utility for me across all issues. To establish a strong anchor and set clear expectations, I will start by proposing my ideal outcome: `valueA` for all issues, targeting my maximum possible utility of 1.00.

The negotiation is very early (t = 0.02). The opponent has offered their apparent maximum twice in a row, which is my absolute minimum (valueE across all issues). As a gesture of good faith to get the negotiation moving without giving up significant value, I will make a small concession on one of my least important issues (issueE) from my optimal preference to the next best, while holding firm on the highest-weighted issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC).

The opponent has made a significant concession early on, particularly on my highest-weight issues (issueA and issueB). Since it is only t=0.04, I should retain a very high utility for myself while reciprocating their movement by conceding slightly on my lower-weight issues (issueD and issueE), signaling a willingness to trade items they seem to value. I will hold firm on issueA, issueB, and issueC.

The negotiation is still very early (t=0.06). The opponent has made significant concessions on issueA and issueB, which are my highest-weighted issues (0.3 each). To reciprocate and encourage further movement toward my utility on A and B, I will hold firm on them (and issueC) while making gradual concessions on my lowest-weighted issues, issueD and issueE.

The opponent has made significant concessions, coming to 'valueA' on our highest priority issue (issueA) and 'valueC' on issueD and 'valueD' on issueE, aligning somewhat with our previous offers. Given that it is still very early in the negotiation (t=0.08), I will lock in the aligned areas (issueA, issueD, issueE) and concede slightly on issueC (moving to 'valueB') to encourage the opponent to improve their offer on issueB. This maintains a strong utility of 0.86 while demonstrating a willingness to negotiate.

The opponent is conceding quite rapidly, and their latest offer gives me a solid utility of 0.78 by aligning on issues A, C, D, and E (partially or fully). Since the time is only t=0.10, I have room to negotiate for a slightly higher utility. I will accept their values for issues A, C, D, and E but maintain a request for my highest-utility value on issue B, aiming for a utility of 0.84.
